results.title: Analyst, Commerce Insights & Analytics (Hershey, PA, US) | Hirevector
highlights:
Analyst, Commerce Insights & Analytics (Hershey, PA, US) | Hirevector
[...]
# Analyst, Commerce Insights & Analytics (Hershey, PA, US)
[...]
Posted May 29, 2026
[...]
- Location : Hershey, PA
- Department : Consumer Connections
- Summary of Role :
[...]
- The Analyst, Commerce Insights & Analytics supports the measurement, reporting, and insight generation for Hershey’s omnichannel commerce and retail media initiatives. This role plays a critical part in connecting the dots between retail media investments, shopper behavior, and sales performance across eCommerce and brick-and-mortar retail environments. The ideal candidate is data-curious, detail-oriented, and eager to support analytics that improve campaign performance, inform strategic decisions, and uncover opportunities to drive conversion at the digital and physical shelf. This position works closely with Commerce, Sales, Media, and Shopper Marketing teams to ensure analytics are integrated into planning and post-campaign evaluation.
[...]
- Performance Reporting & Dashboard: Build and maintain recurring reports and dashboards to track media effectiveness, ROAS, conversion, and shopper engagement across key retail partners. Support updates to internal performance tools using Excel, Power BI, or other visualization platforms
[...]
- Retail Media & Sales Data Integration: Assist in collecting and synthesizing data from RMNs (Amazon, Walmart Connect, Kroger, Instacart, etc.), sales portals, and syndicated sources (Circana, NielsenIQ). Help identify correlations between media activity and retail performance at the brand, campaign, or retailer level
[...]
- Campaign Measurement Support: Contribute to campaign wrap-ups by compiling performance metrics, retailer feedback, and shopper response. Support test-and-learn programs by helping track variables and analyze performance against control groups
[...]
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with Commerce Media, Sales, and Shopper Marketing teams to align on reporting requirements and campaign objectives. Partner with internal teams and agency partners to ensure consistent data inputs and definitions
[...]
- Insights Generation & Communication: Translate data into clear summaries and highlight key insights and implications for internal stakeholders. Help identify opportunities to improve investment efficiency and optimize campaign performance
[...]
- Desired knowledge, skills, and abilities :
[...]
- Strong Excel and analytical skills; familiarity with visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau is a plus
- Understanding of retail media platforms and eCommerce metrics
- Detail-oriented with a passion for accuracy and insight generation
- Strong communication skills, especially in distilling data into clear, actionable takeaways
- Ability to manage multiple workstreams and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
- Experience working with retail data (Amazon Brand Analytics, Walmart Luminate, Circana, etc.) preferred
[...]
- Minimum Education and Experience Requirements :
[...]
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Analytics, Economics, or a related field
[...]
- At least 1-3 years of experience in marketing analytics, eCommerce reporting, or shopper insights
- CPG, agency, or retail experience is a plus

# Staff Engineer, OT Digital Systems
[...]
### Job Description
[...]
Staff Engineer working on digital systems for Hershey Digital Factory initiative. Leading automation solutions and collaborating with cross functional teams for enhanced manufacturing performance.
[...]
#### Responsibilities:
[...]
- Executes digital and automation solutions across Hershey’s manufacturing sites
- Helps implement digital manufacturing solutions in support of the Hershey Digital Factory initiative
- Leads the execution of digital systems and solutions (cost estimates, feasibility, interpretation of requirements specifications and recommendations)
- Provides leadership and expertise in support of projects utilizing AVEVA PI and HiveMQ software suites
- Identifies opportunities to drive the value generation of the digital manufacturing suite of applications
- Coaches and mentors engineers and builds technical capability and leadership
- Collaborates with team members in engineering and IT to deliver measurable value for Hershey
[...]
#### Requirements:
[...]
- Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering, Electrical Engineering Technology, Information Technology, or closely related field
- 8 years’ experience leading and designing digital automation and controls projects
- Advanced knowledge of Connected Worker software platforms including Augmentir
- Programming languages/scripting (IEC 61131-3, C#, Python, SQL, etc.)
- Modern software architectures, including microservices, containers, OSI Model Layers 1-4, cloud computing technologies
- Industrial control system integration using open integration protocols OPC/MQTT Unified Namespace Architectural Standards
- Software State-of-the-art control components and systems
- Strong understanding of industry and business standards

highlights: Category Consultant owns the evolution of “perfect screen” practices across total snacking — including PDPs, taxonomy, online assortment, search, and content — ensuring Hershey’s categories win at the moment of purchase.   The ideal candidate is deeply data‑curious, forward‑thinking, and experimentation‑driven, with a passion for turning insights into scalable solutions. This role is critical to delivering 1–3 year digital shelf strategies and advancing Hershey’s leadership across the CMG Aisle, Salty Aisle, Seasonal Aisle, Transaction Zones, and Protein Category.      Role Responsibilities:  Digital Shelf Strategy & Subject Matter Expertise   Serve as the subject matter expert on the digital shelf, defining best‑in‑class online merchandising standards and solutions that drive scalable, accelerated, and profitable growth for Hershey’s portfolio and retail partners  Own and evolve perfect screen frameworks across total snacking, including product detail pages (PDPs), taxonomy/navigation, online assortment optimization, search, ratings & reviews, and rich content  Develop platform‑specific strategies across Amazon, Walmart, Target, Kroger, and OnDemand retailers, recognizing unique shopper behaviors, algorithms, and execution requirements    Test & Learn Leadership   Lead digital test‑and‑learn agendas in partnership with Category Leadership, Customer Teams, Media, and Insights, identifying high‑impact opportunities across content, assortment, search, taxonomy, and digital media  Design, analyze, and scale evidence‑based tests focused on media effectiveness, conversion, ROI, and shopper engagement across priority categories and retail platforms  Translate test results into clear learnings, recommendations, and scalable playbooks, accelerating speed‑to‑market and continuous improvement    Analytics, Insights & Performance Measurement   Leverage data, insights, research, and experimentation to inform 1–3 year digital merchandising strategies  Synthesize disparate data sources into simple, compelling narratives that drive decision‑making for both internal partners and external customers  Establish success metrics and learning agendas that balance short‑term performance with long‑term capability building    Cross‑Functional & External Collaboration   Partner closely with Sr. Managers of Category Leadership, Consumer/Shopper Insights, Media, and Sales to align digital strategies across the enterprise  Serve as a key contributor to GCIC/MCIC digital merchandising solutions, presenting insights and recommendations as a trusted advisor  Engage confidently with retailers and external partners at all levels, using data and insights to influence strategies and decisions       Desired knowledge, skills, and abilities:   Strong knowledge of search engine optimization, digital merchandising and digital marketing  Ability to collaborate with cross functional partners internally highlights:

[...]
# Sr. HR Analyst, Compensation
[...]
Posted May 29, 2026
[...]
### Location: Hershey, PA
[...]
Work Location: Open to remote candidates though preference will be given to candidates local to Hershey, PA.
[...]
Reports to: Director, U.S. Compensation
[...]
### Summary of Role:
[...]
The Sr HR Analyst Compensation will play a critical role on the Global Total Rewards team and is accountable for translating compensation data into actionable insights. Responsibilities include supporting the business on compensation-related requests, including job evaluations, offers, analyses and other related requests as needed and assigned, as well as job matching, benchmarking survey submissions and results analyses.
[...]
The ideal candidate will have experience in compensation, particularly as a partner to the business and job matching, and a strong understanding of compensation principles, market trends, data analysis techniques, and relevant regulations (ex: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), Minimum Wage).
[...]
- As a compensation partner to the business, works closely with HR Business Partners and business leaders to understand their needs, evaluate and benchmark their roles, and support offer and other award development.
[...]
- Continuously audit data to maintain data integrity and make recommendations based on analyses. Review and maintain job codes and titles in SuccessFactors system to ensure consistency, accuracy and accessibility. Analyze data and reports to identify trends / insights that will influence business decisions.
[...]
- Support job mapping project; identify areas of concern and explain mapping decisions and FLSA implications; maintain mapping files and related documentation. Review job descriptions to assess appropriate market benchmarks and to determine pay level.
[...]
- Participate in compensation surveys. Support the analysis of results and implications to Hershey’s compensation levels and practices when survey results are returned as well as the data integrity and maintenance of Hershey data within our Compensation tool, PayFactors.
[...]
- Support project work, such as reorganizations, redesign of processes for efficiency, and other projects that arise from time-to-time through analysis of data and research of supporting materials.
[...]
- Support compensation year-end program activities, including audits of data and program cost exercises as well as updates to program brochures and knowledge articles.
[...]
- Desired knowledge, skills, and abilities:
[...]
- Effective verbal and written communications skills.
- Ability to apply job evaluation techniques such as market pricing, classification, etc.
- Ability to analyze data, draw conclusions, and problem-solve
- Ability to research complex questions
- Strong Microsoft Excel skills with proficiency in other Microsoft applications, SuccessFactors, and SAP
[...]
- Minimum Education and Experience Requirements:
[...]
- Analysis and Decision Making
[...]
- Systematically gathers information from a variety of sources, analyzes information, identifies implications of data, draws appropriate conclusions, generates viable alternative solutions to a question or problem, and evaluates the consequences of choosing each alternative. This includes recognizing when a problem exists and taking proactive steps to resolve the problem.
[...]
- US Compensation Regulations and Laws

- Monitors federal, state, and local compensation laws and regulations in order to ensure company compliance. Keeps abreast of all relevant laws and emerging regulations.
[...]
- Requirements:
[...]
- Education –
- Bachelor's degree required in Human Resources, Business, Finance, or related field. Experience –
- 3-5 years in Compensation or related experience with proven analytical and computer software capabilities. Progress towards or attainment of CCP preferred.

results.title: Hershey using AI to revolutionise marketing and decision making
highlights:
Hershey using AI to revolutionise marketing and decision makingdefault-output-block.skip-main
[...]
# Hershey’s bets on AI to power growth
[...]
Hershey turns to AI to power growth. (Image: Getty/sandoclr)
[...]
## From marketing spend to manufacturing and innovation, AI is quietly reshaping confectionery
[...]
## Hershey and AI – summary
[...]
- Hershey AI rollout focused on robust backend systems
- Agentic AI analyses hundreds of thousands of marketing inputs
- Real-time insights guide advertising spend amid changing macroenvironment
- AI shifts confectionery decisions from historical data to live signals
- Industry wide AI enables faster innovation efficiency and competitive advantage
[...]
The Hershey Company has, by its own admission, been cautious in its use of AI, particularly when it comes to marketing.
[...]
“Because we know that the output of AI can look impressive. But if you don’t put the time and resources into the back-end capabilities, none of it works,” says Vinny Rinaldi, vice president of consumer connections at Hershey.
[...]
But that’s all set to change, with the launch of the confectionery giant’s new AI-powered systems. As of this month, Hershey has the ability to make fast, informed decisions about where advertising dollars go, “based on what’s actually happening” in the market rather than what happened in the past.
[...]
## Hershey’s AI-powered marketing
[...]
The maker of major brands, including Reese’s Pieces and Twizzlers, has created its own AI system to “ingest and analyse hundreds of thousands of marketing inputs” provided with a partnering analytics platform.
[...]
The platform pulls data from places like social media, search engines, and streaming services. This is then fed into an agentic-AI-powered platform, giving Hershey ”visibility" into all of its sales data.
[...]
“That’s how I start every day,” says Hershey’s Rinaldi. “Enquiring about things like: What are my best and worst performing channels? Where should I move my dollars? What’s driving revenue? It gives me the answers immediately and takes in the context of the current macroenvironment.”
[...]
The approach reflects a wider shift towards using AI as an operational backbone rather than a surface-level tool. By linking data sources and analysing performance continuously, the system enables quicker responses to market movements and changing consumer behaviour.
[...]
In practice, this means less reliance on retrospective reporting and more emphasis on day-to-day optimisation, with AI helping teams prioritise resources, identify opportunities and make decisions grounded in current conditions rather than historical trends.
[...]
As Hershey’s experience shows, the real power of AI in confectionery is not flashier campaigns or novelty tools, but the ability to connect vast, previously siloed data sets and turn them into action at speed.
[...]
Across the sector, companies are using AI to optimise formulations, predict demand spikes around seasonal events, reduce waste, sharpen pricing strategies and better anticipate shifts in consumer sentiment – all critical in a category facing inflationary pressure and rapidly changing tastes.
[...]
What makes this moment different is scale. Large confectioners now have the data infrastructure to apply AI end to end – from cocoa sourcing and factory efficiency to shelf placement and personalisation.
[...]
Smaller players, meanwhile, are accessing similar capabilities through off-the-shelf platforms, lowering the barrier to entry for smarter decision-making. The result is an industry that can respond in near real-time rather than relying on historical assumptions.
[...]
Handled responsibly, AI offers confectionery companies a way to be both more creative and more commercially disciplined, freeing teams to focus on innovation while machines handle complexity.
[...]
What’s more, says Rinaldi, AI will “continue accelerating the pace of change”.

results.title: ‘We should own it’: Hershey’s ad chief on AI agents and MMM
highlights:
‘We should own it’: Hershey’s ad chief on AI agents and MMM
[...]
# ‘We should own it’: Hershey’s programmatic chief on AI agents and media mix modeling
[...]
AI media buying agents steal the headlines, but some of the highest-impact applications of agentic technology by brand advertisers has been in the form of media mix modeling (MMM), systems that monitor the impact of ad investments and help to divine a marketer’s next move.
[...]
Hershey’s has been working with companies like Mutinex and Tracer.tech to speed up media measurement reporting and, in turn, their media investment decisions. The confectioner spent a year developing a system of several AI agents that provide media modeling for campaigns over the course of weeks, not months.
[...]
Vinny Rinaldi, vp of consumer connections at Hershey, said the new approach has enabled the company to prioritize “relevance over reach,” elevate planning and strategic objectives above the cheap thrill of a low CPM, and link media strategy closer to business strategy. It’s also helped steer investment decisions, such as a recent lean into Reddit, and will be informing the advertiser’s approach to the current TV upfront season.
[...]
In this conversation, Rinaldi explained how the strategy had altered its investment choices, Hershey’s thinking around agentic media buying and planning, and where tech should be stopped.
[...]
## Do you expect to start looking at building out buying or planning agents?
[...]
, yes. I think planning is the most important thing that gets overlooked.
[...]
With a modernized MMM, I think you can build a planning agent around hyper-focused data and outputs. We have started to test out what does that look like from a planning perspective.
[...]
From a buying perspective, the same can also be true. You can take all of this data and feed it into a buying agent and/or custom algorithm — which we’ve been actually spending a lot of time and effort on for the last three years. We’ve been one of the early partners of Chalice. We’ve customized a lot of our bidding ecosystem using their data sets and using their integration into a lot of the major DSPs, mainly The Trade Desk, but across YouTube and Meta as well.
[...]
That’s a great question. To make it differentiated for a company like Hershey, inherently, we should own it. I say that because it’s our data fueling it. You might want to pile on top some agency data, but at the end of the day, it becomes our asset because we’re feeding it sales data and other assets that an agency won’t own.
[...]
We own every single technology and data contract. The agency just sits in our buying seats in our ecosystem, but they are still our agents-of-record, and I think it’s a really important distinction because if I didn’t own all of my contracts, we would have never been able to build that infrastructure out the way that we have. [Publicis Groupe has handled U.S. media duties for Hershey’s since 2024.]
[...]
## What were
[...]
It’s funny. You look at what’s happening in the world and everything you read about is agentic-this, or AI-that. The acceleration of ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini into the forefront of everyone’s lives feels like the replacement of what Google Search was back when it first launched. [But] it took years to build to that launch moment [and] the infrastructure that had to be built for those things to work is very undervalued.
[...]
That’s the journey we’ve gone on. A lot of the time that we’ve spent in the last year was building out our infrastructure. It’s like building a home: it can be the most beautiful thing on the outside, but if you forgot to pour the concrete foundation, the first storm is going to blow it over.
[...]
Pouring that foundation is critical to this, to the success of any of these agentic AI capabilities, in my opinion, and I think it’s the first thing that people overlook, because it’s the most unsexy part of the job.

results.title: Hershey’s Daniel Mohnshine on how AI is cutting product development by three months | The Drum
highlights:
Hershey’s Daniel Mohnshine on how AI is cutting product development by three months | The Drum
[...]
# Hershey’s Daniel Mohnshine on how AI is cutting product development by three months
[...]
Between sessions at the judging of this year’s The Drum Awards for Marketing in New York, Daniel Mohnshine, vice-president of brand development and brand creation strategy at Hershey, described an industry where AI is dramatically compressing timelines, accelerating decision-making and forcing marketing leaders to become far more operationally sophisticated than ever before.
[...]
“It’s much more comprehensive than it has ever been,” he said of the modern CMO role. “So that means, in our case, our CMO is focused on innovation, R&D, consumer communications, creative, media – the whole nine yards.”
[...]
customer experience.
[...]
That philosophy matters even more as AI reshapes the pace of modern business. “The speed has become even more important. And that’s speed across all vectors – how you talk to consumers, how quickly you can produce and get things into market. And that refers to both physical products and communication.”
[...]
The biggest change is happening inside Hershey’s innovation pipeline. While much of the public conversation around AI still focuses on content generation, Mohnshine says the more meaningful transformation is happening earlier in the process – in concepting, testing and consumer feedback loops.
[...]
“We use AI in a couple of ways,” he explained. “One is, it’s a great tool for gathering insights. Having insights and understanding what’s coming next is critical to speed.”
[...]
But the real gains are coming in innovation development itself. “Within our innovation pipeline, we will often use AI to concept faster and develop prototypes and get consumer feedback much faster than we have previously.”
[...]
The numbers are already material. “We’ve reduced our innovation pipeline development time, from a concepting standpoint, by three months by leveraging AI,” he said. “And we think it can get faster.”
[...]
Still, he pushed back on the growing narrative that AI will somehow replace marketers altogether. “I would say the biggest myth is that it’s going to replace jobs or somehow replace marketing and the skills required. We still look for people that are highly strategic and understand consumers and can use AI as a tool to amplify skills they already have.”
[...]
For all the AI hype, Mohnshine believes the real competitive advantage may still come from mastering the fundamentals faster than everyone else.
[...]
“There’s a real opportunity to not lose sight of the basics. Getting really, really consumer-centric, using all the tools and data that are available to do it, and then applying the fundamentals really well faster.”
[...]
, the future may
[...]
the loudest brands or
[...]
automated ones, but to the companies capable
[...]
learning quicker than everybody else without forgetting who they are.

results.title: AI and MMM: Hershey's strategy for relevance. - Viral Methods
highlights:
The company spent an entire year developing an Artificial Intelligence system capable of transforming the way it measures, plans, and decides where to invest in media. The idea was never to simply adopt an off-the-shelf tool, but to build something custom — something that connects sales data, campaign performance, and media modeling into a single ecosystem powered by AI agents.
[...]
The person explaining all of this is Vinny Rinaldi, VP of media and marketing technology at Hershey’s, who shared details on how the company is using Media Mix Modeling — the well-known MMM — along with AI agents to make smarter, faster decisions that are more closely tied to actual business outcomes. 🍫
[...]
The answer didn’t come from a ready-made platform or an outside vendor with a packaged solution. It came from within, through a lot of hard work, iteration, and most importantly, data. The company worked with partners like Mutinex and Tracer.tech to build a proprietary framework made up of multiple Artificial Intelligence agents that integrate various data sources. The goal was to understand, in near real time, what’s working and what’s not in active campaigns — something that used to take months to analyze and now happens in a matter of weeks.
[...]
At the core of this framework is Media Mix Modeling, a methodology that analyzes how different media channels — TV, digital, social media, paid search, among others — individually contribute to business results. MMM isn’t new to the industry, but what Hershey’s did was supercharge this approach with layers of Artificial Intelligence that make the process much faster and more precise. Instead of waiting months for a backward-looking analysis, the company can now adjust its strategies dynamically, reacting to market signals before the budget gets wasted on channels that aren’t delivering.
[...]
prove real impact
[...]
quickly discovered is
[...]
One of the most thought-provoking points in the conversation with Rinaldi is about so-called media agents — autonomous Artificial Intelligence systems that can make media buying and planning decisions without direct human intervention on every transaction. This is a space that’s evolving fast in the industry, and a lot of people are still trying to figure out what role agencies, platforms, and advertisers themselves play in this new landscape.
[...]
With a modernized MMM, it’s possible to build a planning agent powered by hyper-focused data and specific outputs. Hershey’s has already started testing this kind of approach.
[...]
On the buying side, the company isn’t standing still either. Hershey’s has been working for the past three years with Chalice, customizing bidding algorithms that integrate with major DSPs, primarily The Trade Desk, but also YouTube and Meta. This isn’t a recent adoption — it’s an incremental build that has been maturing over time.
[...]
technology and data contracts
[...]
The agency — in
[...]
case, Publicis Groupe, which has handled media operations in the United States since 2024 — works within the brand’s ecosystem but doesn’t own the infrastructure. It’s an important distinction. According to Rinaldi, if Hershey’s didn’t have that ownership structure, it never would have been able to build what it has today
[...]
What Hershey’s is building goes beyond a technology project. It’s a new layer of data sovereignty and media intelligence. In a market where platforms have more and more power over how budgets get allocated and how results are reported, having a proprietary Artificial Intelligence system that mediates all of this represents a real competitive advantage.
[...]
’s case shows that adopting Artificial Intelligence in advertising doesn’t have to be a race for buzz
[...]
implementations. When technology is treated as a tool
[...]
of a clear
[...]
— and not as the
[...]
— the results tend to be much more solid and sustainable.

results.title: Hershey cuts product development time by three months using AI, says brand VP
highlights:
Hershey cuts product development time by three months using AI, says brand VP
[...]
# Hershey cuts product development time by three months using AI, says brand VP
[...]
Hershey cut three months from its product development cycle using AI to speed up concepting, prototyping, and consumer feedback. The company's VP says AI won't replace marketers-it amplifies the skills they already have.
[...]
## Hershey cuts product development by three months using AI
[...]
Hershey's vice-president of brand development Daniel Mohnshine says the company has shaved three months off its innovation pipeline by using AI to accelerate concepting, prototyping and consumer feedback loops.
[...]
The acceleration reflects a broader shift across consumer brands where marketing now sits at the center of product development, data strategy and innovation. At Hershey, that means the CMO oversees R&D, consumer communications, creative and media alongside traditional marketing functions.
[...]
Much of the public conversation around AI focuses on content generation. The real gains at Hershey are happening earlier in the process.
[...]
"We use AI in a couple of ways," Mohnshine said. "One is, it's a great tool for gathering insights. Having insights and understanding what's coming next is critical to speed."
[...]
The company applies AI to concept faster and develop prototypes, then collect consumer feedback much faster than before. For a company the scale of Hershey, removing three months from development cycles changes the economics of innovation. It allows teams to test more aggressively, react faster to emerging behaviors and fail earlier before expensive commitments.
[...]
### AI won't replace marketers, but it will change the job
[...]
Mohnshine rejected the narrative that AI will eliminate marketing roles. "The biggest myth is that it's going to replace jobs or somehow replace marketing and the skills required. We still look for people that are highly strategic and understand consumers and can use AI as a tool to amplify skills they already have."
[...]
For all the AI hype, the competitive edge may come from mastering basics faster than competitors. Getting consumer-centric, using available data and applying fundamentals well at speed.
[...]
The future belongs not to the loudest brands or the most automated ones, but to companies capable of learning quicker than everyone else without losing their identity.
[...]
### Hershey cuts product development time by three months using AI, says brand VP

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. You will contribute to technical design decisions, delivery planning, and continuous improvement of our platforms and products.Job responsibilities

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group, we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will contribute to our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at relevant drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Design, implement, and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, writing robust, performance-critical code and contributing to core infrastructure.
[...]
Model Improvement & Reasoning: Develop and execute strategies to systematically improve performance on scientific tasks, including long-horizon task completion and complex reasoning challenges.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into concrete machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria.
[...]
Evaluation & Benchmarks: Design and implement evaluation methodologies to assess model capabilities relevant to biological research, working with domain experts to establish benchmarks and curate high-quality data.
[...]
Research-to-Production: Collaborate closely with researchers to translate ideas and prototypes into scalable, production-ready systems.
[...]
As a Machine Learning Scientist:
[...]
Focus: You focus on the execution of defined projects. You are responsible for writing clean, efficient code to test specific hypotheses regarding reasoning and alignment.
[...]
Engineering: You contribute to the maintenance of the training infrastructure and data pipelines, ensuring experiments run reliably on our clusters.
[...]
Collaboration: You work closely with senior scientists to implement novel algorithms, translating research papers into working prototypes.
[...]
BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, or a related quantitative field with 2+ years of relevant work experience. Or Ph. D. with 0-2 years relevant work experience.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including post-training techniques to enhance domain knowledge, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Strong software engineering skills and experience working with high-performance computing systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data).
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]

highlights: as a trusted partner for organizations across various industries, providing innovative technology solutions that drive growth and efficiency. Committed to excellence, TCS leverages its extensive expertise and a talented workforce to deliver value to clients worldwide. The company emphasizes continuous learning, innovation, and sustainable practices, making it an ideal place for aspiring professionals to grow and develop their careers.About The RoleAs a Gen AI Developer Intern at Tata Consultancy Services, you will be an integral part of our innovative technology team. This role is designed to provide hands-on experience in designing, developing, and testing cutting-edge Generative AI solutions for our esteemed clients. During your internship, you will undergo comprehensive training through TCS's Initial Learning Program, which is a fully paid initiative aimed at equipping you with the necessary skills and knowledge to succeed in the rapidly evolving AI landscape. The internship offers a unique opportunity to work on real-world projects, contribute to proof-of-concept (POC), proof-of-value (POV), and minimum viable product (MVP) development, and gain exposure to the latest AI technologies.At TCS, we believe in nurturing talent and fostering a culture of continuous learning. As part of your internship, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams, including design and development, to translate client requirements into innovative AI-driven solutions. This role also involves analyzing existing systems, designing databases, and customizing applications to optimize operational efficiency for our clients. The position promises a dynamic environment where your technical skills will be honed, and your problem-solving abilities will be put to the test in real-world scenarios.QualificationsTo be considered for this internship, candidates should possess a strong foundation in computer science or related fields. A degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent is preferred. Candidates should have a keen interest in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data analytics. Prior experience or coursework related to Generative AI, natural language processing, or deep learning is advantageous. Strong programming skills in languages such as Python, Java, or C++ are essential. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, along with the ability to work collaboratively in a team environment, are also required. Candidates should demonstrate a proactive attitude, eagerness to learn, and the ability to adapt to new technologies quickly.ResponsibilitiesAs a Gen AI Developer Intern, your primary responsibilities will include collaborating with the design team to convert client requirements into technical designs and AI solutions. You will develop, create, and modify Generative AI applications in accordance with project specifications. Customizing deliverables to meet client needs and optimize operational workflows will be a key aspect of your role. You will analyze existing systems to identify areas for improvement and implement modifications to enhance performance.You will also be involved in designing and managing databases to support AI applications, ensuring data integrity and efficiency. Testing and validating AI models and applications to ensure they meet quality standards will be part of your daily tasks. Additionally, you will participate in team discussions, contribute to documentation, and stay updated on the latest advancements in AI technology. Your role will require a combination of technical expertise, creativity, and effective communication to deliver impactful solutions for our clients.BenefitsInterns at TCS enjoy a range of benefits designed to support their professional growth and well-being. results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]
- Develop across the lifecycle of LLMs including data engineering, pre-training, fine-tuning, post-training, and evaluation
[...]
- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability
[...]
- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD and Roche to solve complex problems in
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.
[...]
- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments
[...]
- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]
- You have experience in developing LLM/GenAI products and are familiar with open-source AI frameworks like Open WebUI.
[...]
- Strong interest in solving problems in drug discovery and biomedical science.
[...]
- You have strong programming skills in Python and using deep learning frameworks like PyTorch
[...]
- You have extensive experience with deep learning & LLMs.
[...]
- An extensive track record of delivering innovative solutions in machine learning
